seoTitle: How to Use Hyperlinks on Canva Presentations
metaDescription: Learn how to add hyperlinks in Canva presentations so viewers can click through to websites, resources, or other slides directly.
intro: Hyperlinks turn a static Canva presentation into an interactive one, letting your audience click straight through to a website, resource, or another part of your slideshow. This is especially useful for business presentations, sales decks, and educational materials. This guide covers how to add and manage those links.

steps.heading: Select the text or element to link
steps.body: Highlight the text, shape, or image you want to turn into a clickable link.

steps.heading: Open the link option
steps.body: Click the link icon in the toolbar, which appears once you've selected an element.

steps.heading: Enter the destination
steps.body: Paste in a website URL, or choose another page within your Canva design if you're linking internally.

steps.heading: Apply and test the link
steps.body: Confirm the link, then use Canva's Present mode to click it and make sure it goes to the right place.

steps.heading: Repeat for other slides
steps.body: Add links to any other elements across your presentation that need to be interactive.

faq.question: Do hyperlinks work when I present in Canva?
faq.answer: Yes, links are clickable when you present directly from Canva or share it as an interactive link.

faq.question: Will hyperlinks still work if I export as a PDF?
faq.answer: Yes, Canva preserves clickable links when you export presentations as PDF.

faq.question: Can I link to another slide in the same presentation?
faq.answer: Yes, Canva lets you select a specific page within your own design as the link destination.

faq.question: Is adding hyperlinks a Pro-only feature?
faq.answer: No, adding hyperlinks to text and elements is available on Canva's free plan.
